industry.headline: Cypress Creek Secures $3.5 Billion for Steel River Energy Center
industry.summary: Cypress Creek Energy, a U.S.-based solar and energy storage project developer, has secured $3.5 billion in financing. This funding is designated for the first two phases of the Steel River Energy Center project. The developer focuses on solar and energy storage solutions to support the project's development and financial requirements for this specific energy center initiative.

industry.headline: Military Engineer Services Issues O&M Tender for 6 MW Solar
industry.summary: Military Engineer Services (MES) has invited bids for the comprehensive operation and maintenance (O&M) of solar projects. The tender covers a 4 MW and a 2 MW solar project located at the Army area in Jodhpur, Rajasthan. These combined 6 MW of projects require professional O&M services to ensure continued operational efficiency within the military installation.

industry.headline: Bihar Regulator Approves Tariffs for 275 MW Rooftop Solar
industry.summary: Bihar Electricity Regulatory Commission (BERC) has approved tariffs discovered through competitive bidding for rooftop solar power. The procurement involves nearly 275 MW of rooftop solar power from approximately 250,000 Kutir Jyoti consumers across Bihar. This regulatory approval facilitates the procurement of solar energy from a large number of residential consumers under the specific Kutir Jyoti scheme.

tech.summary: Sierra Club welcomes the search for asset managers for New York City’s five public pension systems announced by Comptroller Mark Levine and trustees. The search opens a review of major asset managers to ensure strong climate accountability within the city's pension systems.

tech.headline: New cooling panel uses PV and waste cooking oil
tech.summary: Researchers have designed an innovative home cooling panel system. The design incorporates PV power generation, thermoelectric cooling, and latent heat storage using waste cooking oil. Through optimized modeling, results suggest that a single panel can deliver approximately 6–15 kWh/day of cooling, providing a potential new method for residential temperature management using renewable energy.

tech.headline: Chile sets bidding terms for first energy auction including storage
tech.summary: Chile’s CNE has released preliminary terms for the 2026/01 power supply tender. This technology-neutral process favors renewables by excluding high-emission fuels and allowing storage-backed bids. Utilizing 15-year contracts and time-of-day demand blocks, the structure is designed to support photovoltaic and hybrid solar-plus-storage projects, specifically targeting daytime and evening peak coverage requirements.
tech.searchQuery: Chile releases bidding terms for first energy auct 2026

tech.headline: SNEC 2026 shows solar shifting toward system integration and AI
tech.summary: SNEC 2026 highlighted a significant industry shift from module-centric growth toward system integration. Storage and AI are becoming increasingly central to project design and value creation. At the technology level, back-contact technology gained momentum as a premium platform, while emerging applications and scenario-specific modules signaled a trend toward deeper market segmentation within the solar sector.
tech.searchQuery: SNEC 2026 wrap-up: Storage, AI and back-contact ar 2026

tech.headline: Longi develops 27.27% efficient heterojunction back-contact solar cell
tech.summary: Longi and Yangzhou University researchers developed a laser-based manufacturing strategy for HJT back-contact solar cells. This method mitigates shock-wave-induced damage, improving passivation stability and device performance. The approach resulted in a certified 27.27% efficient solar cell, providing an industrially viable pathway toward the fabrication of ultra-high-efficiency photovoltaic technology through improved laser processing techniques.

learn.title: Solar PV and the Photoelectric Effect
learn.intro: Understanding how sunlight becomes usable electricity is the foundation of the solar revolution. The photoelectric effect lets panels turn photons into a steady flow of electrons, powering homes and reducing carbon emissions worldwide.
learn.c1_text: When a photon hits a silicon atom, its energy knocks a bound electron free, creating a charge carrier. Imagine a pool table where a cue ball (photon) hits another ball (electron) and sends it across the table (circuit). Each photon can free one electron, producing current.
learn.c2_text: Free electrons move from the n‑type layer to the p‑type layer, establishing an electric field that separates charge. This built‑in voltage is about 0.5 V per cell; 60 cells in series give ~30 V, enough to drive household appliances after inversion.
learn.c3_text: India installed over 58 GW of solar PV by 2024, driven by the same photoelectric principles. Government targets 280 GW by 2030, making large‑scale farms and rooftop systems crucial for meeting demand.
learn.stat3: US DOE research shows 1 m² panel can produce ~200 kWh/year
learn.stat4: Silicon accounts for >90 % of global PV modules

learn.title: Rooftop Solar & Net Metering in India
learn.intro: Rooftop solar lets households generate clean power on-site, while net metering ensures excess electricity feeds the grid and earns credits—key to India's decentralized clean‑energy push.
learn.c1_text: Panels mounted on roofs absorb sunlight and convert it to DC electricity. An inverter changes DC to AC for home use. A typical 5 kW system on a 100 m² roof can offset ~8,000 kWh annually—roughly the consumption of a four‑person family.
learn.c2_text: When rooftop generation exceeds demand, the surplus flows to the grid, and the utility records a credit at the retail rate. In the next billing cycle, the household draws from those credits, effectively paying only for net consumption.
learn.c3_text: MNRE’s 2022 guidelines mandate 1:1 credit for exported solar energy. States like Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu have set caps of 5 GW for rooftop net metering, spurring over 5 million installations nationwide.
learn.stat2: Average rooftop system payback period: 4‑5 years
learn.stat3: Net metering users saved $2.1 billion in 2023

learn.title: Second‑Life EV Batteries for Grid Storage
learn.intro: When electric‑vehicle batteries retire from cars, they still hold 70‑80 % capacity—ideal for stationary storage. Repurposing them extends value, supports renewables, and cuts waste.
learn.c1_text: After ~8‑10 years in a vehicle, a lithium‑ion pack drops below 80 % of its original range. Instead of recycling, it can be re‑configured into modules that store grid energy, similar to repurposing a used laptop battery for backup power.
learn.c2_text: Modules are re‑tested, re‑balanced, and integrated with battery‑management systems that limit depth‑of‑discharge to 50‑60 %. This prolongs cycle life to 3‑5 years for grid use, delivering 1‑2 MWh per repurposed pack.
learn.c3_text: India’s 2023 EV rollout produced ~120 GWh of used batteries. Pilot projects in Gujarat and Karnataka are installing second‑life packs to smooth solar output, aiming for 500 MWh of storage by 2026.
learn.stat1: Global EV battery waste avoided 2024: 15 GWh
learn.stat2: Cost per kWh for second‑life storage: $120‑$150
learn.stat3: IRENA analysis shows 20‑30 % LCOE reduction with second‑life storage
learn.stat4: India targets 10 GW of stationary storage by 2030

learn.title: Comparing Solar Panel Types
learn.intro: Choosing the right solar panel impacts cost, efficiency, and installation space. Knowing the differences between monocrystalline, polycrystalline, and thin‑film helps stakeholders make informed decisions.
learn.c1_text: Made from a single silicon crystal, these panels reach 22‑24 % efficiency. They perform best in limited roof space—producing ~330 W per 1.6 m² panel, similar to fitting a high‑performance athlete in a small gym.
learn.c2_text: Constructed from multiple silicon shards melted together, they deliver 15‑18 % efficiency. While slightly larger for the same power, they cost 10‑15 % less, making them popular for utility‑scale farms with abundant land.
learn.c3_text: Using materials like CdTe or CIGS, thin‑film panels achieve 10‑13 % efficiency but are lightweight and can be integrated into building facades. India’s 2022 pilot on school rooftops used 1 MW of thin‑film for easy retrofitting.
learn.stat4: NREL predicts 30 % cost drop for thin‑film by 2030

learn.title: Perovskite Solar Cells – The Next Frontier
learn.intro: Perovskite solar cells promise record efficiencies and low‑cost manufacturing, potentially surpassing silicon and accelerating clean‑energy adoption worldwide.
learn.c1_text: Perovskite crystals absorb a broad spectrum, achieving >25 % lab efficiencies in just a few years. Their simple solution‑processing is like printing a solar panel on paper, drastically cutting material waste.
learn.c2_text: The tunable bandgap (1.5‑2.3 eV) allows stacking with silicon in tandem cells, boosting combined efficiency to >30 %. Stability improvements now reach 1,000 hours under 1‑sun illumination, moving toward commercial viability.
learn.c3_text: The Indian Institute of Technology Delhi received a $5 million grant in 2023 to scale perovskite modules for rooftop use. Pilot installations aim for 100 kW by 2025, targeting 15 % cost reduction versus silicon.
learn.stat3: NREL expects perovskite‑silicon tandems to reach 30 % by 2030

learn.title: Geothermal Energy Basics
learn.intro: Geothermal power taps Earth’s internal heat, providing a reliable, low‑carbon source for electricity and heating—essential for baseload power in a renewable future.
learn.c1_text: Temperatures rise 25‑30 °C per kilometer depth. Wells tap hot water or steam at 150‑350 °C, turning turbines directly (dry steam) or via binary cycles where a secondary fluid drives the generator.
learn.c2_text: Dry‑steam plants need >150 °C steam and can achieve 20‑30 % efficiency. Binary plants work with lower temperatures (90‑150 °C) using organic Rankine cycles, expanding usable resources to more sites.
learn.c3_text: The Deccan Traps and Himalayan foothills host >10 GW geothermal potential. The 2022 Indian Geothermal Mission aims to commission 1 GW of capacity by 2030, focusing on district heating in Delhi and power in Gujarat.
learn.stat3: US DOE estimates CO₂ reduction: 0.5 kg per kWh
learn.stat4: India’s geothermal pilot projects: 5 MW total (2024)
